macro_segments.title: The Requirement of a Razor (Ta'ar) for Metzora's Shaving: Debate Between Rabbanan and Rabbi Eliezer

micro_segments.title: Three Categories of People Obligated to Shave: Levi'im, Metzora, and Nazir
Rabbanan's Position: Shaving the Beard is Only Prohibited with a Razor
How Rabbanan Learn Metzora Requires Razor from the Beard Override
Rabbi Eliezer's Position: Beard Prohibition is Broader Than Razor Alone
How Rabbi Eliezer Learns Metzora Requires Razor from Nazir Override
The Principle of Aseh Docheh Lo Ta'aseh and the Conflict Resolution

macro_segments.title: The Function of Rosho and Zekano in Teaching the Metzora's Shaving Overrides

micro_segments.title: What Rabbi Eliezer Learns from Rosho: Prohibitions of Sideburns as Separate Issue
What the Rabbanan Learn from Zekano: Override of Beard Shaving Prohibition
The Distinction Between Beard and Sideburns: Different Scopes of Prohibition
Why Two Verses Are Needed: Teaching Both the Principle and Its Inference

macro_segments.title: The Function of Rosho and Zekano in Teaching the Metzora's Shaving Overrides (continued)

micro_segments.title: Sideburns Are Prohibited Even Without a Razor, Unlike the Beard

macro_segments.title: Sideburns Halakhic Details: Practical Applications and Tosafot's Resolution

micro_segments.title: Tosafot's Inference: Head Shaving is Prohibited Even Without a Razor
Three Sources on Sideburn Prohibition: Gemara, Sifra, and Tosefta
Tosafot's Synthesis: Scissors Like a Razor (K'ein Ta'ar) is Prohibited
Shulchan Aruch's Ruling: Beard Only a Razor, Sideburns More Stringent
Contemporary Practice: Caution Against Even Scissors on Sideburns

macro_segments.title: Requirement of Complete Hair Removal in Giluach: Two Hairs Invalidate the Mitzvah

micro_segments.title: Anyone Shaving Without a Razor or Leaving Two Hairs Has Not Fulfilled the Mitzvah
Rav Acha's Principle: Rubo K'Kulo (Majority as Whole) Versus Yigalchenu (Complete Hair)
Source for Complete Hair Requirement: Yigalchenu by Nazir Tamei on Seventh Day
Tolerance Level: One Hair Remaining is Still 'All,' But Two Hairs is Not
Challenge: Is Yigalchenu Teaching About Nazir Tamei Relevant to Nazir Tahor?

macro_segments.title: Edge Cases of Giluach: Shaving Residual Hair and the Concept of Significant Acts

micro_segments.title: Rava's Question: If One Hair Fell Out and One Was Shaved, Has the Mitzvah Been Done?
The Answer: Cutting Only One Hair When No Others Remain is Not a Significant Act
Giluach Defined as a Process: Multiple Hairs Must Be Involved for Significance
Inference: Even Though the Person Becomes Bald, No Mitzvah is Fulfilled

macro_segments.title: Nazir's Permitted and Prohibited Acts Regarding Hair Care

micro_segments.title: Nazir May Run Fingers Through Hair and Use Hands to Straighten Hair
Unintentional Hair Loss While Straightening Hair: Rabbi Shimon's Leniency
Combing is Prohibited: Intentional Removal of Tangled or Knotted Hairs
Combing and Shabbat: Extension to Hair Removal on Shabbat and Pesik Reisha
Rabbi Ovadia's Position: Modern Combing May Not Be Pesik Reisha

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yishmael's Prohibition: Using Cleansing Agents on Hair

micro_segments.title: Prohibition Against Washing Hair with Earth or Mineral-Based Cleansing Agents
Dispute: Is It the Specific Harmful Agent or All Minerals That Are Prohibited?
Practical Difference: Can a Harmless Mineral be Used Under Each Interpretation?
Unresolved: The Gemara Leaves This as a Teiku (Unresolved Question)
